name: Triangles and Quadrilaterals
whatIsIt: Triangles are named by sides or angles: EQUILATERAL (3 equal sides), ISOSCELES (2 equal), SCALENE (all different); or RIGHT (one 90° angle). Their three angles always add to 180°. QUADRILATERALS have 4 sides: square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium — and their four angles always add to 360°.
whyNeeded: Triangles and quadrilaterals are the shapes of roofs, screens, kites, tiles and trusses. Knowing their properties (equal sides, angle sums) lets you find missing angles and understand stronger, real structures.
realLifeUses: A roof truss is made of triangles for strength
A TV screen and a book are rectangles
A kite is a special quadrilateral
Finding a missing angle in a triangle using the 180° rule

vocabulary.meaning: Triangle angles add to 180°; quadrilateral angles add to 360°.
A quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel.

story.title: The Missing Angle
story.text: Sir draws a triangle with angles 60° and 70°, hiding the third. 'Find it,' he says. Nina remembers the angles of a triangle add to 180°. She adds 60 + 70 = 130, then 180 − 130 = 50. 'The missing angle is 50°!' Sir smiles: 'The angle-sum rule never fails.'
story.extractedProblem: A triangle has angles 60° and 70°. What is the third angle?
story.answerInStory: 50° — because the three angles add to 180° (180 − 130).

visual.caption: Triangle angles total 180°; quadrilateral angles total 360°. Use these to find a missing angle.

teachingGallery.title: Name and use the rules
teachingGallery.note: Sort by sides/angles; use the angle sums.

examples.caption: A right triangle has one 90° angle.
A square is a special rhombus and rectangle.

standardMethod.summary: Classify by sides or angles; to find a missing angle, use 180° for triangles and 360° for quadrilaterals.
steps: Triangle by sides: 3 equal (equilateral), 2 equal (isosceles), none (scalene).
Triangle by angle: a 90° angle makes it right-angled.

workedExamples.problem: A triangle has angles 90° and 45°. Find the third angle.
A quadrilateral has angles 90°, 90°, 100°. Find the fourth.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using 180° for a quadrilateral (or 360° for a triangle).
commonMistakes.fix: Triangle angles add to 180°; quadrilateral angles add to 360°. Count the sides first.
Equilateral = ALL 3 sides equal. Two equal sides = isosceles.

easy.q: The three angles of any triangle add to how many degrees?
A triangle with all three sides equal is…

medium.q: A triangle has angles 50° and 60°. Find the third (in degrees).
The four angles of any quadrilateral add to how many degrees?

challenge.q: A quadrilateral has angles 80°, 100°, 90°. Find the fourth (in degrees).
Ravi uses 180° to find a missing angle in a square. What's the fix?

questions.q: A triangle has angles 90° and 30°. Third angle (degrees)?
A triangle with exactly two equal sides is…
How many degrees do a triangle's angles add to?

teachBackPrompt: Draw a triangle and a four-sided shape for a grown-up. Tear off the corners of the triangle and line them up to show they make a straight line (180°), and explain how you'd find a missing angle in each shape.

revisionCard.summary: Triangles by sides: equilateral (3 equal), isosceles (2), scalene (0); by angle: right (one 90°). Angle sums: triangle = 180°, quadrilateral = 360°. Missing angle = the total minus the known angles.

rememberIt.hook: Triangle angles add to 180; quadrilateral to 360
rememberIt.unpack: Triangles by sides (equilateral/isosceles/scalene) and angles (acute/right/obtuse). Quad angle sum = 360.

studentQuestions.q: Why do a triangle's angles add to 180?
studentQuestions.a: Tear off the three corners and place them together - they form a straight line, which is 180 degrees.

studentQuestions.q: What do a quadrilateral's angles add to?
studentQuestions.a: 360 degrees - it splits into two triangles, 180 + 180.
